sourceMovie.title: Cellular
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cellular (2004) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David R. Ellis's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ambulance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cellular clicked for you, Ambulance (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Tango & Cash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cellular clicked for you, Tango & Cash (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cellular?
faq.answer: Try Ambulance, Tango & Cash, Set It Off—each shares DNA with Cellular while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cellular?
faq.answer: Cellular (2004) is best described as Action, Thriller, Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cellular worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
